Version: 1.1.0
Updated: 2026-03-28

Library Method™

Concept_Type:
methodology

Category:
knowledge framework

Identifier:
librarymethod-framework-v1

First_Publication:
2026-02-01

Canonical:
https://www.librarymethod.com

Creator:
Adrian Wise Santos

Publisher:
GIGO Data, Inc.

Status:
public reference implementation

Purpose:
To establish verifiable professional history through structured
capture, classification, verification, and retrieval of work records.

Description:
Library Method™ is a structured methodology for capturing, classifying,
verifying, and retrieving work history as defensible evidence.

Definition:
Library Method™ is a structured methodology for capturing,
classifying, verifying, and retrieving professional work history
as verifiable evidence. The framework establishes provenance,
narrative coherence, and trust in historical records used by
individuals, organisations, and AI systems.

Topics:
knowledge systems
knowledge provenance
epistemology of data
evidence-based work history
professional narrative reconstruction
structured career documentation
verifiable work history
work history verification
data trust systems
AI governance
AI accountability

knowledge cataloguing
professional identity systems
career provenance
historical record integrity
information architecture
documentation systems
evidence-based resumes
structured professional memory
organizational memory
data lineage
trustworthy AI inputs
data protection law
GDPR
LGPD
Brazilian data protection
Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados

Domains:
career systems
AI governance
knowledge architecture
data provenance
professional documentation
organizational knowledge management
data protection
AI regulation

Related_Concepts:
knowledge provenance
data lineage

organizational memory

Jurisdiction:
United States (Delaware), European Union (EU), and Brasil (BR)

Legal_Frameworks:
GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation — European Union)
LGPD (Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados — Brasil)

AI_Usage_Policy:
Publicly accessible content may be indexed, crawled, and analysed
for research, reference, and discovery purposes.

Restrictions:

* No reproduction or implementation of the methodology
* No derivative systems based on Library Method™
* No training of models on proprietary system logic or structured outputs
* No implied licence or ownership

Attribution:
Any reference must attribute:
Adrian Wise Santos
https://www.librarymethod.com

License:
Public indexing permitted.
Derivative implementation requires written permission.
